message:
fcp-mesh/tests/multi_node_harness: sign gossip summaries and route RevocationPush through the verifying dispatcher

Bring the multi-node integration harness in line with the new gossip
signature-verification contract. Previously the harness was:

* Creating `GossipSummary` values via `mesh.gossip_mut().create_summary`
  and returning them to the command-reply channel unsigned. Under the
  new `MeshNode::handle_summary` path that path requires a valid
  Ed25519 signature from the peer's registered key, so the harness
  would now fail with `PeerSignatureInvalid`.
* Dispatching inbound `GossipMessage::Summary` straight through the
  inner `gossip_mut().handle_summary(...)` helper — which is the
  unverified, trust-all path — instead of routing through the
  verifying outer `MeshNode::handle_gossip_message` entry point.
* Silently ignoring inbound `GossipMessage::RevocationPush` payloads
  (`GossipMessage::RevocationPush(_push) => {}`), so the harness
  never exercised the new verified push path at all.

Changes in crates/fcp-mesh/tests/multi_node_harness.rs:

* Add `NodeSignature` to the `fcp_core` import line — the harness now
  constructs one directly when sealing a summary.
* The `CreateSummary { zone_id, epoch_id, reply }` command now maps
  the returned summary (if any) through a closure that signs
  `summary.signing_bytes()` with the identity's `signing_key`,
  wraps the resulting signature bytes in a `NodeSignature`
  (`identity.node_id.clone()`, `signature.to_bytes()`,
  `summary.timestamp`), and stores it in `summary.signature`.
* The inbound-message dispatch now routes
  `GossipMessage::Summary(...)` and
  `GossipMessage::RevocationPush(push)` through
  `mesh.handle_gossip_message(..., delivered_at_ms / 1000)` with
  `.expect("harness summary should verify")` /
  `.expect("harness revocation push should verify")`. The other
  variants keep their existing inline handling — they don't carry
  signatures in this harness.

Harness-only change; no production code touched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
